violations.detail: Back contains a dash joining two facts: 'It marks where Cas9 can bind — Cas9 cannot cut without an adjacent PAM'. The dash adds a second fact. Should be split into two cards or trimmed to one fact.
Back says 'It is error-prone, often introducing insertions or deletions' — the comma + participle adds a consequence as a second fact. Should be split or trimmed.

Cards 6 (NHEJ for knockout) and 7 (HDR for insertion) form a mirror pair — if only two repair pathways are presented and you know one does knockout, the other must do insertion. Should merge into a multi-cloze or drop one.

Most cards are definitional/factual (what is X, what does X do). Only card 6 and arguably card 10 are why/how/application cards. That's ~20% rather than the ~40% target. Several factual cards could be reframed as why/how cards.

violations.rule: Specific constant value without understanding why
violations.detail: Card 5 asks for the length of guide RNA (~20 nucleotides) — this is a specific constant value. A better card would ask WHY ~20 nucleotides is the right length (specificity vs. off-target effects) or what happens if it's too short/long.
